name: Subtype Polymorphism
def: Code written against a supertype works with any subtype (substitutability).
problem: Write logic against an abstraction and let any conforming concrete type plug in.
mechanism: A subtype may be used wherever its supertype is expected; dynamic dispatch selects the actual implementation at runtime (via vtables/interfaces).
limits: Dynamic dispatch cost; harder to inline
whenNot: Closed sets of variants (use sum types/pattern matching)
myths: "Inheritance = subtyping" — interfaces give subtyping without implementation inheritance
